steps.title: Read the roots from the numerator
steps.content: Shortcut (Vieta's): The fraction equals zero only when its numerator equals zero. The numerator $(x-6)(x-11)$ is already factored, giving roots $x = 6$ and $x = 11$. The excluded value $x = 5$ is neither root, so both are valid.

steps.title: Apply sum of roots = -b/a
steps.content: Sum of roots: $6 + 11 = 17$. Equivalently by Vieta's, the numerator expands to $x^2 - 17x + 66$, so sum $= -(-17)/1 = 17$. That confirms C is correct.
